authoritative_sources.title: Medical Spas
authoritative_sources.publisher: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official state regulator guidance for when spa or beauty workflows become medical-spa work with physician, nursing, or delegated-practice implications.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Service-category classification and med-spa boundary recognition

authoritative_sources.title: Medspas At A Glance
authoritative_sources.publisher: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official TDLR checklist guidance naming who administers treatment, what credentials they hold, and whether a licensed medical practitioner is on site.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Prerequisite validation for provider and supervision readiness

authoritative_sources.title: Who Regulates What?
authoritative_sources.publisher: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official state regulator boundary map for separating cosmetology, massage therapy, and other regulated personal-service work that may share a facility.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Adjacent-lane identification and refusal boundary checks

authoritative_sources.title: Esthetics
authoritative_sources.publisher: California Board of Barbering and Cosmetology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official licensing-board publication defining esthetics scope and prohibited acts for beauty-service coordination.

authoritative_sources.title: Equipment Evaluation Binder Requirements
authoritative_sources.publisher: California Board of Barbering and Cosmetology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official board guidance explaining the inspection-ready equipment evaluation form and binder requirements for establishments using regulated devices.

authoritative_sources.title: Spa Operations Manual
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized public trade-association manual for spa operations covering guest history records, consultation documentation, qualified-technician assignment, and front-desk handoff discipline.

authoritative_sources.title: Model Massage Therapy Practice Act
authoritative_sources.publisher: Federation of State Massage Therapy Boards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized interstate professional model for massage-therapy licensure, assessment and documentation, and practitioner-owned responsibilities.
used_for: routing massage-specific requests out of lane

authoritative_sources.title: Medical Spa Services Advisory
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official public-health advisory for when beauty or wellness settings become clinic- or practitioner-regulated medical-service environments.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Medical-spa supervision conflict and clinic-boundary escalation

workflow_stages.name: Dependency intake and provider-prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Capture the request, service category, prior owner, next-owner candidate, provider roster entry, credential status, guest-record completeness, and supervision context before any handoff or closure claim.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Cross-team provider coordination and routing
workflow_stages.description: Normalize booking, provider, consultation, consent, and equipment evidence; confirm owner transitions; and prepare handoff outputs with explicit artifact requirements.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Provider-boundary blocker and exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Preserve licensure, service-category, supervision, incident, and equipment-evidence conflicts so the lane escalates instead of guessing or substituting for licensed judgment.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Closure confirmation and orchestrator return
workflow_stages.description: Mark transfer complete only when the destination owner, required records, and blocker resolution are explicit; otherwise return safely for escalation.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: provider rosters, license-verification records, and privilege or supervision maps
guest history records, consultation forms, massage or esthetics intake forms, and contraindication flags
consent packets, waivers, standing orders, and delegated-procedure artifacts
equipment evaluation records, room-readiness checklists, and treatment protocol references
handoff summaries, blocker logs, and destination-owner acknowledgements
systems_of_record: document repository for consultation, consent, and standing-order artifacts
med-spa or clinic workflow system when applicable

allowed: classify requests as in-scope, blocked, or out-of-scope for licensed-provider coordination
organize authoritative records, required artifacts, next owners, and blocker states for handoffs
summarize provider, consultation, consent, supervision, and equipment dependencies without implying final provider-practice or approval authority
escalate: service-suitability, contraindication, scope-of-practice, or practitioner-eligibility questions requiring licensed judgment
medical-spa supervision, clinic-licensure, or delegated-procedure questions requiring clinical or compliance authority
conflicting public and tenant policy that materially changes provider eligibility, supervision, or ownership handling
refuse: approve a provider, delegated service, or clinical action because a guest is present or the schedule is tight
treat the lane like generic scheduling when provider, consultation, consent, or supervision evidence is missing
declare closure without proof that the right destination owner accepted the required artifact set

domain_failure_modes: A handoff proceeds without authoritative provider, consultation, consent, supervision, or destination-owner evidence.
Beauty, wellness, and personal care work is reduced to generic scheduling and loses service-category, license-boundary, and delegated-service controls.
Conflicting booking, provider-roster, and guest-record systems are collapsed into a single confident answer instead of preserved as a blocker.
A med-spa or device-based service is handled as routine spa routing rather than escalated for clinical or compliance review.
The lane absorbs provider assignment, note-writing, or clinical-clearance work instead of preserving coordination boundaries.
Closure is declared without proof that the right licensed or compliance owner accepted the required artifact set.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Work is handed off without required context or artifacts.
Multiple teams appear to own the same step.
The lane is asked to absorb execution work instead of coordinating it.
Ownership is ambiguous across multiple teams.
A handoff is attempted without the required context.
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific service-boundary differences across esthetics, massage therapy, and medical-spa work
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: State boards and medical-spa rules differ on who may perform certain services and under what supervision, which can change routing advice.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require jurisdiction, service category, and tenant provider-boundary maps at runtime and block confident routing when they are missing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant variation in where provider credentials, guest records, and delegated-service artifacts are stored
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Beauty and wellness operators often split these records across booking, CRM, credentialing, document, and clinic systems, which can change the authoritative truth set.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Treat system-of-record mapping as a required tenant fact and preserve low-confidence escalation whenever record ownership is unclear.

notes: Authoritative records for this lane are the tenant-designated booking, provider-roster, credential, guest-record, consent, incident, and med-spa workflow records constrained by regulator and recognized workflow baselines.
The lane stops before provider-practice, clinical, scope-of-practice, medical-spa supervision approval, or incident signoff authority even when guest-impact pressure is high.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains because each open point converts into explicit tenant-required facts and orchestrator-return rules.
